sect . x. the second case concern the person to who the oath be make , whether he who have swear the performance of a thing unto another , the party unto who he swear be decease , be bind to make it good unto the heir or successor of the say party ? i answer , ordinary he be . it be certain the party swear be oblige , if he express that he will perform the oath unto the heir of the other . it may also be take for grant , that he be bind though he express it not , if the oath take relate to dignity ; because dignity vary not with the change of person . whence if any subject or soldier swear fidelity unto his king or general , the oath be to be take as make unto they also who succeed unto that dignity . the same may be say in matter of debt , and sundry other thing wherein consideration of duty , or contract give occasion of the oath . if you shall inquire how it come to pass , that the bond of a oath be personal as to the party swear , be not also personal as to the party unto who the oath be make , but pass unto his heir , or successor : or which come to the same matter , how it come to pass that a man may engage himself unto another , and his successor , though successor be not express in the oath ; but can oblige himself , and his successor , though they be express in the oath . i answer , the reason of the difference lie in this , that in the one case obligation of a man self be mean , in the other , obligation of other . any man may oblige himself spiritual as he will or please , and therefore may by his proper act oblige himself , as well to the successor of another as to the person himself ; but a man can lay a obligation upon another unless he also consent , and therefore he can by his act spiritual oblige himself only . now whereas i say in answer unto the doubt in this case , that the party swear be ordinary oblige ; the reason why i say so be , because it may be that sometime he be not oblige ; for see that the intention of the party swear , aught to be judge of accord to the nature of the thing , and subject matter , where from the nature of the thing promise , and other circumstance it may probable be conjecture , that the party swear intend only a personal promise unto the person unto who he swear , and not unto his successor , the obligation of the oath divolve not unto those successor . sect . xi . but of active cause this may suffice , i pass to the impulsive , which be partly external , and partly internal . internal , when a man through the mere motion of his own will , not compel by any other , free offer himself to take a oath , or through some transportation of anger , love , or other passion of a perturb mind , or through delight in sin , and impious custom of swear rash , and without judgement , besprinkle his discourse with oath . which vice , both in respect of the heaviness and frequency of the sin , i can wish be more often , and vehem•ntly reprehend in sermon , as i see it be diligent , and sharp do in his time by the most devout man , John Chrysostome , leave by the just judgement of God , through oath the earth mourn , and the lord swear in his wrath , that he will not hold they guiltless , who so contemn his dreadful name , that they fear not to invoke his most sacred majesty as witnesse• , and arbiter without any necessity . but i shall not say much concern oath of this kind . all spontaneous oath , be absolute forbid , except upon weighty and necessary occasion . it will be worth our while , to hear Augustine of himself ; A. the duty towards man which i learn from the consideration of God goodness and mercy be this , that we shall be patient towards all man , not render evil for evil to any man 1 Thes. 5. 14 , 15. , but do good to all Gal. 6. 10. , even to they that hate we Mat 5. 44. and to be merciful as our father also be merciful , and kind to the unthankful and to the evil luke 6. 35 , 36. , and forgive one another , even as God for Christ sake have forgive we Eph. 4 32. Colos. 3. 13. . Q. in what especial be the kindness , love and mercy of God manifest to we ? A. in this be manifest the love of God to we , in that he send his only beget son into the world , to be the propitiation for our sin , and not for we only , but also for the sin of the whole world , that we may live through he . encage John 4. 9 , 10. and 2 2. and 4. 14. Q. do the goodness and grace of God in the gospel give security to sinner , or shall we continue in sin that grace may abound ? A. God forbid Rom. 6. 1 , 2 , 15. ; for the goodness of God lead we to repentance Rom. 2. 4. , and the grace of God that bring salvation teach we that deny ungodliness and worldly lust , we shall live sober , rightcous and godly in this present world Titus 2. 11 , 12. .

Sect. 1. but for the better confirm of this so important a truth , in these atheistical time , see some reason to confirm it . as , first , if the sun , which be but a creature , be so bright and glorious , that no mortal eye can look upon the brightness of it , how glorious then be the creator himself ? or that light from whence it receive its light ? if the frame of the heaven and globe of the earth be so glorious , which be but the low house , or rather the footstool of the almighty , as the holy ghost phrase it , Isa. 66. 1. Mat 5. 35. act 7. 49. how glorious and wonderful be the maker thereof , and the city where he keep his court ? or if sinner , even the worst of wicked man , and god enemy , have here in this earthly pilgrimage , such variety of enjoyment to please their very sense , as who can express the pleasurable variety of object for the sight ; of meat and drink to satisfy and delight the taste , of voice and melodious sound , to recreate the hear ; of scent and perfume , provide to accommodate our very smelling , of recreation and sport , to bewitch the whole man : and the like of honour and profit , which be idol that carnal man do mighty dote upon , and take pleasure in : ( though these earthly and bodily joy be but the body , or rather the dregs of true joy ) what think we must be the soul thereof , viz. those delight and pleasure , that be reserve for the glorify saint , and god dear darling in heaven ? again , second , if natural man find such pleasure and sweetness in secular wisdom , lip-learning , and brain-knowledge ; for even mundane knowledge have such a show of excellency in it , that it be high affect both by the good and bad ; as , oh the pleasure that rational man take therein ! it be so fair a virgin that every clear eye be in love with she ; so rich a pearl , that none but swine do despise it : yea , among all the tree in the garden , none so take with rational man as the tree of knowledge ( as Satan well know , when he set upon our first parent ) insomuch that Plato think , in case wisdom can but represent it self unto the eye , it will set the heart on fire with the love of it . and other affirm , that there be no less difference between the learned and the ignorant , than there be between the live and the dead , or between man and beast . and yet the pleasure which natural and moral man take in secular and mundane knowledge and learning , be nothing comparable to that pleasure , that a experimental christian find in the divine and supernatural knowledge of God word : which make David and Solomon prefer it before the honey and the honeycomb for sweetness : and to value it above thousand of gold and silver ; yea , before pearl and all precious stone for worth . how sweet then shall our knowledge in heaven be ? for here we see but dark , and as it be in a glass , or by moonlight ; but there we shall know , even as we be know , and see God and Christ in the face , 1 Cor. 13. 12. third , if mere naturians have be so take with the love of virtue , that they think if a virtuous soul can but be see with temporal eye , it will ravish all man with love and admiration thereof ; yea , if the very worst of man , drunkard , blasphemer , and the like ; though they most spiteful scoff at , and backbite the people of God ; yet when they know a man sincere , upright , and honest , can choose but love , commend , and honour he in their heart ; as it fare with Herod touch John , and king Agrippa touch Paul . Sect. 2. or rather if god own people be so ravish with the grace and privilege which they enjoy upon earth , as the assurance of the pardon of sin , the peace of a good conscience , and joy of the holy ghost ; which be but glorification begin : what will they be , when they shall enjoy the perfection of glory in heaven ? as see but some instance of their present enjoyment here below . first , if we be never to receive any reward for those small labour of love , and duty we do to the glory of God , and profit of other , we may think our self sufficient recompense in this life , with the calm and quietness of a good conscience , the honesty of a virtuous and holy life ; that we can do and suffer something for the love of Christ , who have do and suffer so much to save we ; that by our work the majesty of God be magnify , to who all homage be due , and all service too little . for godliness in every sickness be a physician , in every contention a advocate , in every doubt a Schoolman , in all heaviness a preacher , and a comforter unto whatsoever estate it come , make the whole life as it be a perpetual Hallelujah . yea , God so shed his love abroad in our heart by the holy ghost , that we be in heaven before we come thither . insomuch , that as the fire fly to his sphere , the stone hasten to the centre , the river to the sea , as to their end and rest , and be violent detain in all other place ; so be the heart of God people , without their maker and redeemer , their last end and eternal rest and quietness , never at rest : like the needle touch with the lodestone , which ever stand quiver and tremble until it enjoy the full and direct aspect of the northern pole . but more particular : how do the assurance of the pardon of sin alone , clear and calm all storm of the mind , make any condition comfortable , and the worst and great misery to be no misery ? to be deliver of a child , be no small joy to the mother : but to be deliver from sin , be a far great joy to the soul . but to this we may add the joy of the holy ghost , and the peace of conscience , otherwise call the peace of God which pass all understanding . these be privilege that make Paul happy in his chain of iron , than Agrippa in his chain of gold : and Peter more merry under stripe , than caiapha upon the judgmentseat ; and Stephen the like under that shower of stone . pleasure be we , if we be Christ : whence those expression of the holy ghost , the lord have do great thing for we , wherefore we rejoice . be glad in the lord , and rejoice you righteous , and shout for joy all you that be upright in heart . let all that put their trust in thou rejoice , let they even shout for joy . rejoice evermore ; and again i say , rejoice . rejoice with joy speakable , and full of glory . our rejoice be this , the testimony of our conscience . your heart shall rejoice , and your joy shall no man take from you , etc so that it be a shame for the faithful , not to be joyful ; and they sin if they rejoice not , whatsoever their condition be . the eunuch no soon feel the pardon of sin , upon his be baptize into the faith of Christ , but he go on his way rejoice , act 8. 39 he then find more solid joy , than ever he have do in his riches , honour , and great place under Candace , queen of the aethiopian . at the same time when the disciple be persecute , they be say to be fill with joy , and with the holy ghost , act 13. 52. and as their affliction do abound , so their consolation do abound also , 2 Cor. 1. 5. for these be comfort that will support and refresh a child of God in the very midst of the flame , as the martyr find : for maugre all their persecutor can do , their peace and joy do exceed their pain ; as many of they manifest to all that see they suffer . Sect. 3. where observe , before we go any further , what sot they be , that cry out , it be in vain to serve God , and unprofitable to keep his commandment , as it be in Malachy 3. 14. for have these fool but taste the sweet comfort that be in the very work of piety , and that heaven upon earth , the feast of a good conscience , and joy of the inward man , they can not so speak . yea , then will they say , there be no life to the life of a christian . for as the priest of Mercury , when they be their fig and honey , cry out , oh how sweet be truth ! so if the worst of a believer life in this world be so sweet , how sweet shall his life be in that heavenly Jerusalem and holy city , where God himself dwell , and where we shall reign with Christ our bridegroom , and be the lamb wife ? which city be of pure gold , like unto clear glass ; the wall of jasper , have twelve foundation garnish with all manner of precious stone ; the first foundation be jasper , the second Saphir , the three a Calcedony , the four a emerald , the five a Sardonyx , the six a Sardius , the seven a chrysolite , the eight a Beryl , the nine a topaz , the ten a Chrysoprasus , the eleven a jacynth , the twelve a amethyst : have twelve gate of twelve pearl , the street thereof of pure gold , as it be transparent glass : in the midst of which city , be a pure river of the water of life , clear as crystal , and of either side the tree of life ; which bear twelve manner of fruit , yield her fruit every month ; the leaf whereof serve to heal the nation : where be the throne of God and of the lamb ; who we his servant shall for ever serve , and see his face , and have his name write in our forehead . and there shall be no night , neither be there need of the sun , neither of the moon to shine in it : for the glory of God do lighten it , and the lamb be the light thereof . into which nothing that defile shall enter ; but they alone which be write in the lamb book of life ; as be express , rev. 21. and 22. Chap. the holy ghost speak after the manner of man , and accord to our slender capacity , for otherwise no word can in any measure express the transcendency of that place of pleasure . only here we have a taste , or earnest-penny , one drop of those divine dainty , of those spiritual , supernatural , and divine pleasure , reserve for the citizen of that heavenly Jerusalem ; some small smack whereof we have even in the barren desert of this perilous peregrination . God let out as it be , a certain kind of manna , which in some sort refresh his thirsty people , in this wilderness , as with most sweet honey , or water distil from out of the rock . as what else be those jubilee of the heart ; those secret and inward joy which proceed from a good conscience , ground upon a confident hope of future salvation ? as what else do these great cluster of grape signify , but the fertility of the future land of promise . Sect. 4. true it be , none can know the spiritual joy and comfort of a christian , but he that live the life of a christian , John 7. 17. as none can learn the virgin song , but they that sing it , rev. 14. 3. no man can know the peace of a good conscience , but he that keep a good conscience . no man know the hide manna , and white stone , with a new name write in it , but they that receive the same , rev. 2. 17. the world can see a Christians outside , but the rapture of his soul , the ravish delight of the inward man , and joy of the spirit for the remission of his sin , and the infusion of grace , with such like spiritual privilege more glorious than the state of kingdom ; be as a cover mess to man of the world . but i may appeal to any man conscience , that have be soften with the unction of grace , and true taste the power of the world to come ; to he that have the love of God shed abroad in his heart by the holy ghost ; in who soul the light of grace shine , whether his whole life be not a perpetual Hallelujah , in comparison of his natural condition ? whether he find not his joy to be like to the joy of harvest ? or as man rejoice when they divide a spoil ? Isa. 9 3. whether he find not more joy in goodness , than worldling can do , when their wheat , wine , and oil abound , psalm 4. 7. and 53. 17. yea , he can speak it out of experience , that as in profane joy , even in laughter the heart be sorrowful : so in godly sorrow , even in weep , the heart be light and cheerful . the face may be pale , yet the heart may be calm and quiet . so saint Paul , as sorrow , yet always rejoice , 2 Cor. 6. 10. our cheek may run down with tear , and yet our mouth sing forth praise . and so on the contrary , where ( oh God ) there want thy grace , mirth be only in the face , 2 Cor. 5. 12. well may a careless worldling laugh more , as what will soon make a man laugh than a witty jest , but to hear of a inheritance of a hundred pound a year , that be fall to a man , will make he more solid merry within . light be sow to the righteous , and joy for the upright , Psal. 97. 11. my servant , say God , shall sing and rejoice : but they shall weep , etc Isai. 65. 14. indeed we be not merry enough , because we be not Christians enough , because sin be a cool of our joy , as water be of fire . and like the worm of Jonah his gourd , bite the very root of our joy , and make it wither : yea , sin like a damp , put out all the light of our pleasure , and deprive we of the light of God countenance , as it do David , Psalm 51. 12. and 4. 6. so that the fault be either ; first , in the too much sensuality of a christian , that will not forgo the pleasure of sin , or the more muddy joy and pleasure of this world , which be poison to the soul , and drown our joy , as bee be drown in honey , but live in vinegar . Men will have spiritual joy , but withal they will not part with their carnal joy : yet this be a infallible conclusion , there be no enjoy a worldly paradise here , and another hereafter . or second , the fault be in the taste , not in the meat ; in the folly of the judgement , not in the pearl , when a grain of corn be prefer before it . to taste spiritual joy , a man must be spiritual ; for the spirit relish the thing of the spirit ; and like love his like . between a spiritual man , and spiritual joy , there be as mighty a appetite and enjoy , as between fleshly meat , and a carnal stomach . therefore the want of this taste and apprehension condemn the world to be carnal , but magnify the joy spiritual , as be above her carnal apprehension . or , third , herein lie the fault ; few feel these joy in this life ; because they will not crack the shell , to get the kernel : they will not pare the fruit , to eat the pulp ? nor till the ground , to reap the harvest . they fly the war , and thereby lose the glory of the victory ▪ they will not dig the craggy mountain to find the mine of gold : nor prune the vine , therefore enjoy not the fruit . they fly mortification , and therefore attain not the sweet spiritual consolation , which ever attend the same . and so much for the reason . the use may be manifold .

Sect. 2. or rather if god own people be so ravish with the grace and privilege which they enjoy upon earth , as the assurance of the pardon of sin , the peace of a good conscience , and joy of the holy ghost ; which be but glorification begin : what will they be , when they shall enjoy the perfection of glory in heaven ? as see but some instance of their present enjoyment here below . first , if we be never to receive any reward for those small labour of love , and duty we do to the glory of God , and profit of other ; we may think our self sufficient recompense in this life , with the calm and quietness of a good conscience , the honesty of a virtuous and holy life : that we can do and suffer something for the love of Christ , who have do and suffer so much to save we : that by our work the majesty of God be magnify ; to who all homage be due , and all service too little . for godliness in every sickness be a physician , in every contention a advocate , in every doubt a Schoolman , in all heaviness a preacher , and a comforter unto whatsoever estate it come ; make the whole life as it be a perpetual Halleluja . yea , God so shed his love abroad in our heart by the holy ghost , that we be in heaven before we come thither . insomuch , that as the fire fly to his sphere , the stone hasten to the centre , the river to the sea , as to their end and rest ; and be violent detain in all other place ; so be the heart of god people , without their maker and redeemer , their last end and eternal rest and quietness , never at rest : like the needle touch with the lodestone , which ever stand quiver and tremble until it enjoy the full and direct aspect of the northern pole . but more particular : how do the assurance of the pardon of sin alone , clear and calm al• storm of the mind ; make any condition comfortable , and the worst and great misery to be no misery ? Psal. 4.7 and 53.17 . yea , he can speak it out of experience , that as in profane joy , even in laughter the heart be sorrowful : so in godly sorrow , even in weep the heart be light and cheerful . the face may be pale , yet the heart may be calm and quiet . so saint Paul , as sorrow , and yet always rejoice , 2 Cor. 6.10 . our cheek may run down with tear , and yet o•• mouth sing forth praise . and so on the contrary , where ( oh God ) there want thy grace , mirth be only in the face ; few feel these joy in this life ; because they will not crack the shell to get the kernel : they will not pare the fruit , to •at the pulp ; nor till the ground , to reap the harvest . they •lie the war , and thereby lose the glory of the victory . they will not dig the craggy mountain , to find the mine of gold . nor prune the vine , therefore enjoy not the fruit . they •lie mortification , and therefore attain not the sweet spiritual consolation , which ever attend the same . and so much for the reason . the use may be manifold .
